meta.name: SageReasoning Stoic Brain
meta.description: A structured, machine-readable and human-readable reference for Stoic philosophy. Designed as the single source of truth for virtue-based reasoning at sagereasoning.com. This file can be loaded directly into an AI agent's context or queried via the SageReasoning API.
meta.status: v1.0 — Phase 1 complete. Scoring rules and API endpoints to be added in Phase 2.
meta.license: SageReasoning Proprietary Licence v1.0 — see /LICENSE for terms
primary_sources: Diogenes Laertius, Lives of the Eminent Philosophers Book 7 (~3rd c CE)

foundations.core_premise: The only genuine good is virtue (arete). The only genuine bad is vice. Everything else — health, wealth, reputation, life itself — is indifferent (adiaphoron). Flourishing (eudaimonia) is achieved by living in accordance with reason and nature, expressed through the four cardinal virtues.

dichotomy_of_control.description: The foundational Stoic principle — Epictetus, Enchiridion 1. What matters morally is only what is 'up to us' (eph' hêmin). Everything else is 'not up to us' and should be treated as indifferent.
dichotomy_of_control.implication_for_scoring: An action is scored primarily on the quality of the agent's intention and reasoning, not solely on the outcome. A sage who acts virtuously but achieves a bad outcome is still scoring near 100.

the_sage.description: The Stoic ideal of the perfectly wise person (sophos). A theoretical benchmark, not a claim about any real person. The Stoics acknowledged the sage was almost never achieved in practice — its purpose is as a standard for direction of travel.
characteristics: Acts always from the four virtues simultaneously — they are unified
Is not disturbed by externals — neither excessive joy nor grief
Has no false beliefs — all their judgements are correct
Is free in the deepest sense — internal freedom from passion
Is a citizen of the cosmos — their concern extends to all rational beings
Experiences eupatheiai (good emotional states) rather than pathê (passions): joy not pleasure, caution not fear, wishing not desire
the_sage.contrast_with_non_sage: All non-sages are considered foolish (phauloí) to varying degrees — but moral progress (prokoptos) is both possible and the central practical goal of stoic philosophy.

flourishing.definition: Living and acting in accordance with reason and nature. Not a feeling of happiness but the objective quality of a life fully expressed through virtue. Seneca: 'The happy life is one that is in accordance with its own nature.'
flourishing.stoic_vs_popular: Popular usage treats flourishing as wealth, pleasure, or success. Stoic flourishing is completely internal — it is available to anyone, including the slave (Epictetus), the sick (Marcus Aurelius), or the condemned (Socrates).
flourishing.how_scored: The SageReasoning score is a measure of the degree to which an action or pattern of actions expresses the four virtues. A high score = movement toward flourishing. A low score = movement away from it.

virtues.note: See virtues.json for the full virtue and sub-virtue definitions with scoring weights.

indifferents.note: See indifferents.json for preferred, dispreferred, and neutral indifferents with virtue relevance scores.

scoring_rules.note: See scoring-rules.json — to be built in Phase 2.

planned_endpoints.description: Returns all indifferents with categories and virtue relevance
Submit an action description, receive virtue scores and sage alignment tier
Returns a user's historical action scores and trend

api_interface.ai_agent_instructions: To use the Stoic Brain for reasoning: review this file and virtues.json + indifferents.json for a conceptual overview of the framework. For live scoring with full virtue weights and sub-virtue analysis, use the SageReasoning API. Start with POST /api/guardrail to check any action, or POST /api/score-iterate to begin a deliberation chain. API key required — request at zeus@sagereasoning.com.
